content:
Stop Memorizing Linux Commands. Start Understanding Them.

21 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Nonmembers click here
Every backend engineer eventually opens a terminal.
It doesn’t matter whether you’re deploying a Spring Boot application, debugging a Docker container, restarting an Nginx service, or checking why your API suddenly started returning 500 errors. Sooner or later, you’ll SSH into a Linux server and rely on the command line.
The interesting part?
Most engineers don’t use hundreds of Linux commands. They repeatedly use the same set of reliable commands that help them inspect systems, troubleshoot production issues, and deploy applications with confidence.
When I first started working with Linux servers, I thought I needed to memorize dozens of commands. In reality, I discovered that mastering a small collection of commands was enough to handle most day-to-day backend tasks.
This article isn’t another list copied from a Linux manual.
Instead, these are the commands you’ll actually see backend engineers, DevOps engineers, and SREs using every single day.
